plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here is my complete plan. The plan file has been written to `/Users/timpietrusky/.claude/plans/joyful-plotting-peach.md`.

---

## Summary: Optimizing `src/pipeline.ts`

**Only one file needs to change.** Each function has a clear algorithmic bottleneck, fixed by switching to hash-map-based O(1) lookups:

| Function | Current Complexity | Fix | New Complexity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `groupByCategory` | O(n²) | `Map<category, CategoryGroup>` — O(1) lookup, `+=` instead of `reduce` | O(n) |
| `findDuplicates` | O(n³) | Single pass into `Map<key, Transaction[]>`, emit buckets with ≥2 entries | O(n) |
| `calculateRunningBalance` | O(n·m²) | Filter once → sort once → single cumulative-sum pass | O(n + m log m) |
| `topSpenders` | O(n² + u²) | Single aggregation pass into `Map<userId, {total, count}>` + native `.sort()` | O(n + u log u) |
| `searchTransactions` | O(n²) | Compile `RegExp` once before the loop; `Set<string>` for O(1) dedup | O(n) |
| `processAll` | — | No change needed; benefits automatically from the above | — |

**All correctness invariants are preserved:** rounding (`Math.round(balance * 100) / 100`), regex special-char escaping, key format (`"userId|amount|date"`), sort order (date ascending), and dedup-by-id behavior are all kept identical.

**Verification:** `npm test` — all 20 correctness tests + all 6 performance benchmarks should pass.
